title: SNAP PROGRAM - 211info
text: HOOD RIVER SELF-SUFFICIENCY, CHILD WELFARE, AND VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION SNAP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides monthly food benefits to help people buy food. Funds are issued through an Electronic Benefit Transfer (EBT) card, also called the Oregon Trail Card. 1610 9th Court Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Eligibility is determined on a case-by-case basis. Individuals and families who: -Live in Oregon -Household income must be at or below 200% of the federal poverty level, based on monthly income before taxes -Are US citizens or eligible non-citizens Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 386-3199 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 386-3199 Main phone Self-Sufficiency Office (541) 386-2962 Main phone Child Welfare Office (971) 673-8870 Main phone Vocational Rehabilitation Office Main phone (541) 386-3199 Alternate (800) 699-9075 ONE Customer Service Center INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply and complete an interview with an ODHS eligibility worker. Applications can be submitted online, by phone, in person, or using a paper application. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: No documents are required to submit an application, however, people may be asked to provide the following: -Identification -Proof of income -Social Security numbers for everyone in your household who wants benefits -Proof of immigration status for eligible non-citizens LANGUAGES: Phone: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement Applications: Available in English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, Somali AREA SERVED: Hood River County Services: Food Stamps/SNAP Other Services Offered At This Location: EMPLOYMENT - VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD ABUSE REPORTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE SERVICES MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HOOD RIVER SELF-SUFFICIENCY, CHILD WELFARE, AND VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION
